_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, comprising an average of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice prices indexed to 2015 at 100, currently stands at 141, meaning prices are about 41% higher than in 2015. This rise has been uneven and has largely occurred in recent years rather than steadily over time. The index reflects global commodity prices, indicating broad pressures across several staple breakfast items. However, the increase is not uniform across all components.
notes.movers: The most significant price increases are seen in coffee, which is up 99% since 2015, followed by orange juice at +34%, wheat at +19%, and sugar at +13%. These gains are driven primarily by weather-related factors and crop diseases affecting delicate soft commodity markets—such as frost and drought for coffee-producing arabica regions and citrus greening plus hurricanes impacting orange juice crops. These are specialized, thin markets where such shocks lead to sharp price fluctuations rather than reflecting broad inflationary trends.
notes.why: The headline rise in breakfast commodity prices is largely due to a few weather-vulnerable crops in thin global markets. Because these are supply-driven issues tied to environmental factors rather than general economic inflation, elevated prices could persist for multiple years depending on future crop conditions and disease management.
